statements.impact_statement: Zarf links containerd only through Helm v3's OCI registry client, which imports the client-side github.com/containerd/containerd/remotes package. Zarf does not run containerd's CRI plugin, manage container lifecycle, or invoke the restart-monitor logger, so the vulnerable CRI label-handling path is not in Zarf's runtime execution path.

Zarf links containerd only through Helm v3's OCI registry client, which imports the client-side github.com/containerd/containerd/remotes package. Zarf does not run containerd's CRI plugin or perform runtime container setup, so the vulnerable supplementary-group parsing path is not in Zarf's runtime execution path.

golang.org/x/crypto/openpgp is unmaintained and flagged unsafe by design regardless of usage. govulncheck's reported call chain shows Zarf's signing path (src/pkg/signing/cosign.go CosignSignBlobWithOptions -> cosign's SignBlobCmd -> sigstore-go pkg/sign.Bundle/GetTransparencyLogEntry) reaching rekor's PGP public-key parser (pkg/pki/pgp.NewPublicKey -> golang.org/x/crypto/openpgp ReadKeyRing/ReadEntity/addUserID/VerifyUserIdSignature). This is a static-analysis over-approximation: rekor's pki.NewArtifactFactory dispatches to one PKI implementation via an explicit Format argument (pkg/pki/factory.go), and sigstore-go hardcodes that format to x509 for every hashedrekord transparency-log entry it creates (pkg/sign/transparency.go: artifactProperties.PKIFormat = string(pki.X509)). Zarf never supplies or accepts PGP-format keys in its signing flags, so the PGP branch of rekor's format dispatch, and therefore the vulnerable openpgp code, is never exercised at runtime.

Zarf links containerd only through Helm v3's OCI registry client, which imports the client-side github.com/containerd/containerd/remotes package. Zarf does not run containerd's CRI plugin, restore CRI checkpoints, or invoke CDI handling, so the vulnerable checkpoint-restoration path is not in Zarf's runtime execution path.

Zarf links containerd only through Helm v3's OCI registry client, which imports the client-side github.com/containerd/containerd/remotes package. Zarf does not run containerd's CRI plugin or import CRI checkpoints, so the vulnerable checkpoint image-reference handling and local image-cache path are not in Zarf's runtime execution path.

Zarf links containerd only through Helm v3's OCI registry client, which imports the client-side github.com/containerd/containerd/remotes package. Zarf does not run containerd's CRI plugin or restore CRI checkpoints, so the vulnerable CRI checkpoint log-restoration path is not in Zarf's runtime execution path.
